question.text: Long hedgers (long futures, selling in spot) benefit when:

answer.text: Basis weakens — futures price rises relative to spot price
Basis strengthens — spot rises relative to futures

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Long hedger is long futures + selling in spot. When basis weakens (futures gain more than spot), the futures leg outperforms → net benefit. **The Trap:** Strengthening basis benefits SHORT hedgers. Equal movements have no hedging benefit. Volatility change alone doesn't determine hedge benefit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers hedger P&L based on basis changes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The basis-hedger relationship: Short hedgers benefit from strengthening, Long hedgers from weakening — memorize this table for exam and practical use.
